text: Continuous integration (CI) is a software development practice where team members regularly merge their code changes into a shared main branch. The process relies heavily on automated builds, meaning that every time new code is pushed, the system automatically compiles it. This is followed by running tests to verify that the new code doesn't break any existing features. By catching issues early, developers can fix bugs quickly before they become bigger problems. CI pipelines handle this entire workflow automatically, from building to testing to reporting results. This approach saves time, improves code quality, and helps teams work more efficiently together.
